briefdescription.para: Stride for each transform (`size1_ * size1_).
Pointer to frequency-domain data.
Number of time samples per transform.
Logical size (stride = size1_ * size1_).
Pointer to time-domain data.
FFTW backward plan.
FFTW forward plan.
Copy constructor.
Constructor.

detaileddescription.para: Performs a forward FFT (time domain → frequency domain). This is because plan_back_ was created with FFTW_FORWARD.

para.formula: $ X[k] = \sum_{n=0}^{N_{ft}-1} x[n] \cdot \exp(-2\pi i \cdot k \cdot n / N_{ft}) $

detaileddescription.para: Note: This is the standard DFT definition (no scaling).
Performs an inverse FFT (frequency domain → time domain). This is because plan_for_ was created with FFTW_BACKWARD.

para.formula: $ x[n] = \sum_{k=0}^{N_{ft}-1} X[k] \cdot \exp(+2\pi i \cdot k \cdot n / N_{ft}) $

detaileddescription.para: Note: FFTW does NOT normalize this, so the result will be scaled by $ N_{ft} $ . You may need to manually divide by $ N_{ft} $ if you want the exact inverse.

briefdescription.para: Get $ {\tt val} = A(t) $ from this fft_array $ A $ at time or frequency index $ t $ (with periodic wrapping).

detaileddescription.para: Only valid when $ \text{size1}\_ = 1 $ (scalar case). If $ t < 0 $ or $ t \geq N_{ft} $, it is automatically wrapped periodically: $ t \equiv t \bmod N_{ft} $ in $ [0, N_{ft} - 1] $.

parameterdescription.para: Whether to read from time or frequency array (fft_domain::time or fft_domain::freq).

briefdescription.para: Get $ M = A(t) $ from this fft_array $ A $ at or frequency time index $ t $ (with periodic wrapping).

detaileddescription.para: If $ t < 0 $ or $ t \geq N_{ft} $, it is automatically wrapped periodically: $ t \equiv t \bmod N_{ft} $ in $ [0, N_{ft} - 1] $.

parameterdescription.para: Matrix to fill with the values at time index $ t $.
Whether to read from time or frequency array (fft_domain::time or fft_domain::freq).

memberdef.argsstring: (const fft_array &B, std::complex< double > alpha, fft_domain type)
memberdef.name: incr

briefdescription.para: Increment this fft_array $ A $ by another scaled array $ B $: $ A \to A + \alpha \cdot B $.

parameterdescription.para: Whether to operate on time or frequency arrays (fft_domain::time or fft_domain::freq).

briefdescription.para: Multiply each time/frequency matrix of this fft_array $ A $ from the left by a complex matrix $ M $: $ A(t) \to M \cdot A(t) $.

parameterdescription.para: The matrix to left-multiply with (must be size1_ × size1_)
Whether to apply to time or frequency domain data (fft_domain::time or fft_domain::freq).

briefdescription.para: Multiply each time/frequency matrix of this fft_array $ A $ from the left by the hermitian conjugate of a complex matrix $ M $: $ A(t) \to M^\dagger \cdot A(t) $.

parameterdescription.para: The matrix to left-multiply with (must be size1_ × size1_)
Whether to apply to time or frequency domain data (fft_domain::time or fft_domain::freq).

briefdescription.para: Copy assignment operator.
Print the time and frequency arrays to a plain text file.

detaileddescription.para:
Outputs:
Header: # Nft size1_ precision
Blocks: time and frequency (each with one line per $ t $).

parameterdescription.para: Number of digits for real/imaginary part (default: 12).

briefdescription.para: Read the time and frequency arrays from a plain text file written with print_to_file.

detaileddescription.para: Resizes internal arrays if needed using the provided FFTW_FLAG.

parameterdescription.para: FFTW planning flag to use if resizing (default: FFTW_ESTIMATE).

briefdescription.para: Read the fft_array data (time and frequency arrays) from an HDF5 group.

detaileddescription.para: If the stored array dimensions differ from the current object, automatically resizes the object using the provided FFTW_FLAG.

parameterdescription.para: FFTW planning flag to use if resizing (default: FFTW_ESTIMATE).

briefdescription.para: Resize the fft_array and rebuild FFTW plans.

detaileddescription.para: Frees any existing memory and FFTW plans, then allocates new arrays and creates new FFTW plans for the given sizes. After calling, the array is reset and ready for use.

parameterdescription.para: New number of time samples per transform.

briefdescription.para: Multiply each time/frequency matrix of this fft_array $ A $ from the right by a complex matrix $ M $: $ A(t) \to A(t) \cdot M $.

parameterdescription.para: The matrix to right-multiply with (must be size1_ xsize1_)
Whether to apply to time or frequency domain data (fft_domain::time or fft_domain::freq).

briefdescription.para: Multiply each time/frequency matrix of this fft_array $ A $ from the right by the hermitian conjugate of a complex matrix $ M $: $ A(t) \to A(t) \cdot M^\dagger $.

parameterdescription.para: The matrix to left-multiply with (must be size1_ × size1_)
Whether to apply to time or frequency domain data (fft_domain::time or fft_domain::freq).

briefdescription.para: Set this fft_array $ A $ to $ A(t) $ = $ M $ at time or frequency index $ t $ (with periodic wrapping).

detaileddescription.para: If $ t < 0 $ or $ t \geq N_{ft} $, it is automatically wrapped periodically: $ t \equiv t \bmod N_{ft} $ in $ [0, N_{ft} - 1] $.

parameterdescription.para: Whether to apply to time or frequency array (fft_domain::time or fft_domain::freq).

briefdescription.para: Set this fft_array $ A $ to $ A(t) = \tt{val} $ at time or frequency index $ t $ (with periodic wrapping).

detaileddescription.para: Only valid when $ \text{size1}\_ = 1 $ (scalar case). If $ t < 0 $ or $ t \geq N_{ft} $, it is automatically wrapped periodically: $ t \equiv t \bmod N_{ft} $ in $ [0, N_{ft} - 1] $.

parameterdescription.para: Whether to apply to time or frequency array (fft_domain::time or fft_domain::freq).

memberdef.argsstring: (int i1, int i2, const fft_array &B, int j1, int j2, fft_domain type)
memberdef.name: set_matrixelement

briefdescription.para: Set one matrix element's time or frequency series of this fft_array $ A $ from another fft_array $ B $.

detaileddescription.para: This sets $ A(i_1, i_2)(t) = B(j_1, j_2)(t) $ across all times or frequencies.

parameterdescription.para: Whether to operate on time or frequency arrays (fft_domain::time or fft_domain::freq).

briefdescription.para: Set all elements of either the time or frequency array to zero.

parameterdescription.para: Indicates whether to clear the time or frequency array.

briefdescription.para: Scale this fft_array $ A $ in-place by a complex scalar $ \alpha $: $ A \to \alpha \cdot A $.

parameterdescription.para: Indicates whether to scale time or frequency array (fft_domain::time or fft_domain::freq).

briefdescription.para: Write the fft_array data (time and frequency arrays) to an HDF5 group.

detaileddescription.para: Stores attributes for Nft_, size1_, and element_size_, and datasets for the time_ and freq_ arrays as 3D arrays of shape [Nft_, size1_, size1_].

briefdescription.para: Destructor.
A class that manages multiple strided FFTs using FFTW.

detaileddescription.para: The data is arranged such that each FFT has stride element_size_ = size1_ * size1_, and is of length Nft. Forward and backward plans are created once and reused.
